subject: [PATCH 2/4] memcg: uint16_t for nr_bytes in obj_stock_pcp

reviews.author: David Laight
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David Laight pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where stock->nr_bytes is already at U16_MAX and a refill occurs, which would cause an overflow when adding nr_bytes to it.

Reviewer noted that the size of local_trylock_t lock depends on kernel build options and suggested moving the uint16_t field to a different location in the struct
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, 19 May 2026 22:31:20 -0700
Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev> wrote:

---

You might want to move it to this hole.
The size of 'lock' depends on kernel build options.

-- David

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the field order needs to be rearranged for better packing, promised to restructure in the final patch (v2), and asked reviewer to check the 4th patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks. In the final patch, I am rearranging the fields for better packing.
Please take a look at 4th patch and see if it still need fixing.

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the memcg kernel module to use a uint16_t instead of an unsigned int for storing nr_bytes in struct obj_stock_pcp. This change is made to reduce memory usage by shrinking the per-CPU cache on 64-bit machines. The patch also includes logic to handle cases where the accumulator would exceed the maximum value of uint16_t, ensuring that it stays within safe limits.
